{"id": 172, "timestamp": "2026-01-01T14:56:42.195603", "source": "agent", "message": "Running command: grep -n -A 10 -B 5 "readUInt32LE" /app/vm.js", "action": "run", "tool_call_metadata": {"function_name": "execute_bash", "tool_call_id": "toolu_84", "model_response": {"id": "0217672793943260e290995bd2535df6af5f05331d9ca561273a6", "created": 1767279402, "model": "kimi-k2-250905", "object": "chat.completion", "system_fingerprint": null, "choices": [{"finish_reason": "stop", "index": 0, "message": {"content": "Now I can see the issue! The instructions are being read in the wrong byte order. Look at the instruction at 0x4005d4: `0x747c9c27`. When we extract the opcode (bits 31-26), we get 0x1d.
